scenario: Meridian Brokerage runs Camunda 8.8 on the order-process model with 8000 orders per day at the brokerage. After the operator starts an instance with orderId 1234 and completes Initiate Payment within 4 minutes, the team must choose the right correlationKey on the published payment-received message so it correlates with that exact instance on the broker.
question: Which correlationKey value on the published payment-received message correlates with the running instance carrying orderId 1234 in this case?

options.text: The correlationKey value must be set to the broker-generated process instance key on the order-process instance
The correlationKey value must be set to the bpmnProcessId order-process on the published payment-received message
The correlationKey value must be set to the deployment id of the order-process model on the published payment-received message
The correlationKey value must be set to 1234, the orderId variable set on the running order-process instance on the broker

a.text: Incorrect. The documented command uses --correlationKey=1234, which matches the orderId variable on the instance rather than the broker-generated process instance key; correlation happens on the configured correlation key, not the engine instance key.

b.text: Incorrect. bpmnProcessId names the process definition and is shared across every instance, so it cannot pick a single running instance; correlation uses the correlationKey value that matches the catch event's correlation key expression.

c.text: Incorrect. Deployment ids identify a deployment batch and cannot pick a single running instance; correlation of the payment-received message uses the correlationKey value tied to the instance's correlation key expression.

d.text: Correct. The documented step runs ./bin/zbctl --insecure publish message payment-received --correlationKey=1234, where 1234 is the orderId set on the instance, so the message correlates with that specific running process instance.

explanation: The Operate guide publishes a payment-received message with --correlationKey=1234, where 1234 is the orderId set on the instance when it was created; this correlation key value targets the specific running instance so it advances past the Payment Received catch event.
